violations.description: FakeEventPublisher, FakeDeadLetterPublisher, InMemoryCommandStatusStore, and FakeDomainServiceInvoker are owned by the collection-scoped fixture and accumulate state across every test in the [Collection("DaprTestContainer")]; there is no per-test Reset() invoked between tests, so accumulated topics, published events, and command status records leak across tests within the same fixture lifetime.
violations.suggestion: Add a Reset() helper on the fixture (and on FakeEventPublisher/FakeDeadLetterPublisher/InMemoryCommandStatusStore) and invoke it from each test class constructor or via an IDisposable per-test wrapper that runs before each test.

violations.description: A static Lazy<Task<IConnectionMultiplexer>> RedisConnection is initialized once per AppDomain and never disposed; the multiplexer leaks across tests, classes, and even fixture lifecycles, and any state cached in Redis between Tier 2 runs is observable to subsequent tests.
violations.suggestion: Move the multiplexer into the DaprTestContainerFixture (IAsyncLifetime) so it is created/disposed with the collection, or wrap each access in a using block; flush the relevant Redis keys in DisposeAsync.

violations.description: Constructor and Dispose both null-out EVENTSTORE_ADMIN_URL/TOKEN/FORMAT without first capturing the prior process value; if the developer or CI sets these env vars before invoking dotnet test, those values are silently destroyed for the rest of the process lifetime.
violations.suggestion: Mirror the snapshot/restore pattern used in GlobalOptionsBindingProfileTests.cs: capture _origUrl/_origToken/_origFormat in the constructor and write them back in Dispose.

violations.description: InitializeAsync mutates EnableKeycloak / ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT / DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT before BuildAsync/StartAsync; if any of those steps throws, xUnit does NOT call DisposeAsync (per xUnit semantics), so the env vars leak into any downstream collection executed in the same process.
violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch (mirroring AspirePubSubProofTestFixture), restore the env-var snapshot in the catch block, then rethrow.

violations.description: Same xUnit IAsyncLifetime hazard: 4 env vars (EnableKeycloak, ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT, DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT, EventStore__SampleFaults__MalformedProjectResponse) are mutated before the topology starts; failure during BuildAsync/StartAsync skips DisposeAsync and leaks them.
violations.suggestion: Adopt AspirePubSubProofTestFixture's SnapshotAndSet/RestoreEnvironmentSnapshot helpers and wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch for guaranteed restoration.

violations.description: Three env vars (EnableKeycloak, ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT, DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT) are mutated before BuildAsync/StartAsync; Keycloak realm import in particular often fails on slow CI hardware, and that failure path leaks the env vars because xUnit skips DisposeAsync.
violations.suggestion: Adopt the SnapshotAndSet/RestoreEnvironmentSnapshot pattern and a try/catch around InitializeAsync.
violations.code_snippet:
Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("EnableKeycloak", "true");
... // 5-min Keycloak startup may throw; DisposeAsync never runs

violations.description: Mutates EnableKeycloak before a 5-minute Keycloak startup; if WaitForEndpointAsync, WaitForTokenAcquisitionAsync, or any health probe throws, xUnit does not call DisposeAsync and EnableKeycloak=true leaks to subsequent collections (potentially forcing them to start unwanted Keycloak containers).
violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch and restore EnableKeycloak in the catch path before rethrowing.

violations.description: DAPR_HTTP_PORT/DAPR_GRPC_PORT are set after capture, but the prerequisite/start-host/sidecar startup that follows can throw (Redis unreachable, daprd path missing, port conflict); on failure xUnit skips DisposeAsync and the in-process Dapr Actors runtime resolves to the abandoned ports for the rest of the test run.
violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch and explicitly restore the two DAPR_* env vars before rethrowing.

violations.description: Same hazard as the EventStore DAPR fixture: DAPR_HTTP_PORT/DAPR_GRPC_PORT are mutated before VerifyPrerequisitesAsync/StartTestHostAsync/StartDaprSidecar; a throw from any of those skips DisposeAsync.
violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch with explicit env-var restoration before rethrow.

violations.description: Test creates HttpClient via _factory.CreateClient() but never disposes it; the WebApplicationFactory tracks created clients, but the test class itself is not IDisposable so handles leak across the class fixture lifetime.
violations.suggestion: Implement IDisposable on the test class and dispose the HttpClient in Dispose, or use a using statement.
violations.code_snippet:
HttpClient client = _factory.CreateClient();
// No using, no Dispose

violations.description: [CollectionDefinition("SignalRRedisBackplaneProofTests", DisableParallelization = true)] disables parallelism without an inline justification comment; the rationale (Redis backplane port-isolation under concurrent runs) lives only in the test file at line 412 and is easy to lose during refactor.
violations.suggestion: Add a /// <summary> comment on the collection class explaining the Redis-backplane parallelism hazard, mirroring the docstrings on AspireTopologyCollection.

violations.description: LogCapturingFactory.LogProvider accumulates entries across tests in the class fixture; tests rely on calling _factory.LogProvider.Clear() at the top of each test to avoid bleed-over. xUnit serializes tests in the same class fixture, so this works, but is fragile if [Theory]/parallel collection is added.
violations.suggestion: Either expose a per-test scope (IDisposable that wraps LogProvider) or migrate to a per-test factory; at minimum add a comment that any test added must invoke Clear() first.

recommendations: Adopt AspirePubSubProofTestFixture's SnapshotAndSet/RestoreEnvironmentSnapshot helper as a shared TestEnvScope utility, and refactor every IAsyncLifetime fixture that mutates env vars (AspireContractTestFixture, AspireProjectionFaultTestFixture, KeycloakAuthFixture, AspireTopologyFixture, DaprTestContainerFixture, TenantsDaprTestFixture) to wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch with restore-on-failure to close the xUnit-skips-DisposeAsync hazard.

Add a Reset() method to FakeEventPublisher/FakeDeadLetterPublisher/InMemoryCommandStatusStore in src/Hexalith.EventStore.Testing/Fakes/ and call it from DaprTestContainerFixture between tests (e.g., expose a fixture.ResetCollectedState() that test class constructors invoke). This converts the current implicit isolation-via-unique-IDs into explicit isolation.

Move the static Redis ConnectionMultiplexer in EventPersistenceIntegrationTests.cs into DaprTestContainerFixture (or a shared Tier 2 RedisFixture) so it is created and disposed deterministically, and add a Tier 2 setup hook that flushes the EventStore-related Redis keys at fixture init (not full FLUSHDB which would clobber other suites).

Mirror GlobalOptionsBindingProfileTests' env-var snapshot pattern in GlobalOptionsTests (and any future env-var-mutating unit tests) so previous values are preserved across the test process. Consider a small EnvSnapshot helper in Hexalith.EventStore.Testing to make this trivial.

Add /// <summary> XML doc comments on every [CollectionDefinition], especially those with DisableParallelization = true, explaining why parallelism is disabled and what shared resource is being protected (Redis backplane, port range, AppDomain cache, etc.). This makes future maintainers' intent-preservation cheap.
summary: Test-isolation discipline is generally strong: 56 fixture/lifetime use sites, the gold-standard AssemblyScanner.ClearCache pattern is consistently mirrored, all temp paths are GUID-suffixed, and AspirePubSubProofTestFixture demonstrates the correct env-snapshot+try/catch pattern. The two material gaps are (1) DaprTestContainerFixture's collection-shared FakeEventPublisher/CommandStatusStore that never reset between tests plus a static undisposed Redis multiplexer, and (2) seven IAsyncLifetime fixtures that mutate env vars before BuildAsync/StartAsync without try/catch, leaking those env vars whenever Aspire/DAPR startup throws.
